role_summary: Civil Rights History Project interviewee; her testimony in this corpus's embedding clusters with the Mississippi rural movement cohort.
ai_reading: McCullar's top semantic neighbors are Clifford D. Browner Jr. (entry 23, count 60), Juadine Henderson (entry 68), Rosie Head (entry 111), Peggy Jean Connor (entry 96), and the John Dudley joint interview (entry 64). The cluster is tightly Mississippi-rural-movement. Her position on the Tactical Pragmatism vs Strategic Vision axis is +0.89, very far toward Strategic Vision, the second-highest reading toward that pole in the audited subset of the catalog (after Rosie Head at +0.80 and Lisa Anderson Todd at +0.72). For an interviewee whose neighbor counts are modest (peak 60) and whose cultural placement appears to be local-rural, the strongly strategic-vision reading is unexpected; the embedding may be catching retrospective long-arc framing in her testimony over the immediate-action specificity her neighbor pattern would predict.
biographical_paragraph: Johnnie Ruth McCullar's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 65 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. Independent biographical sourcing is pending; the strongest signal currently available for movement role is the embedding pattern visible in the AI's reading above.
